intent.design_goal: Develop a functional robotic arm gripper suitable for general-purpose manipulation tasks.
unknowns: gripper_configuration (parallel, perpendicular, three-finger, tactile sensor integration)

requirement_spec.design_goal: Develop a functional robotic arm gripper suitable for general-purpose manipulation tasks.

requirements.description: Design object type resolved as robotic_arm_gripper
downstream_design_consequences: Initializes constraint graph even when numeric targets are absent

requirement_spec.completeness_rationale: All required engineering decisions resolved from prompt.

functional_analysis.primary_function: Develop a robotic arm gripper that can perform general‑purpose manipulation tasks with reliable actuation, sensing, control, and thermal management.

functions.name: Acquire Gripper Status
functions.description: Collect real‑time information about finger position and contact force using embedded sensors.

functions.name: Compute Control Laws
functions.description: Implement algorithms that convert sensor data into desired actuation commands for force and position regulation.

functions.name: Transmit Actuation Commands
functions.description: Relay control signals from the controller to the motor driver and actuators.

functions.name: Actuate Gripper Fingers
functions.description: Convert electrical power into mechanical torque that moves the fingers through the desired trajectory.

functions.name: Distribute Torque via Transmission
functions.description: Transfer the motor torque through belts, gears or linkages to the fingertip mechanisms.

functions.name: Regulate Grasping Force
functions.description: Adjust actuation command in real time to maintain a target contact force while preserving desired position.

functions.name: Maintain Thermal Balance
functions.description: Control heat dissipation from motors and electronics to keep operating temperatures within limits.

functions.name: Execute Grasping Movement
functions.description: Execute the coordinated kinematic motion of the gripper fingers to grasp, hold, and release objects.

functions.name: Provide Power
functions.description: Supply regulated electrical energy from the robot’s power system to all electronic and electromechanical components.

flows.description: Electrical energy from the power source is routed to the actuator to generate torque.
Mechanical torque produced by the actuator is transmitted through the drive assembly to the gripper fingers.
Sensor data on finger position and force is fed to the control algorithm.
Computed command signals are transmitted to the motor driver.
Actuation commands guide the mechanical movement of the fingers.
Force feedback is used by the controller to adjust the grasping force.
Power is supplied to cooling systems to dissipate heat generated by motors and electronics.
Mechanical torque from the transmission drives the finger kinematics during grasp execution.

required_assemblies.name: Actuation Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Deliver precise mechanical movement to the gripper fingers.

required_assemblies.name: Power Distribution Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Regulate and distribute electrical energy to all subsystems.

required_assemblies.name: Control Electronics Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Compute actuation commands and relay them to the actuators.

required_assemblies.name: Sensor Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Provide real‑time feedback on position and force.

required_assemblies.name: Thermal Management Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Control temperature of motors and electronics to avoid overheating.

required_assemblies.name: Gripper Mechanism Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Coordinate finger motion to perform grasp, hold, and release.
